milestones.title: BackRub Project Begins
milestones.description: Larry Page and Sergey Brin start BackRub, a search engine that analyzes the web's link structure.
milestones.longDescription: While PhD students at Stanford University, Larry Page and Sergey Brin began working on BackRub, a search engine that used backlinks to determine website importance. This PageRank algorithm would become the foundation of Google Search.

milestones.title: Google.com Domain Registered
milestones.description: The google.com domain name is officially registered, marking the birth of the Google brand.
milestones.longDescription: The domain google.com was registered on September 15, 1997. The name 'Google' was a play on the word 'googol,' a mathematical term for the number 1 followed by 100 zeros.

milestones.title: Google Inc. Founded
milestones.description: Google is officially incorporated as a company in a friend's garage in Menlo Park, California.
milestones.longDescription: Google Inc. was officially founded on September 4, 1998, in Susan Wojcicki's garage in Menlo Park, California. The company received its first investment of $100,000 from Sun Microsystems co-founder Andy Bechtolsheim.

milestones.title: Google AdWords Launches
milestones.description: AdWords is introduced, allowing advertisers to create text-based ads for targeted placement.
milestones.longDescription: Google AdWords (now Google Ads) launched in October 2000, revolutionizing online advertising with a self-service platform that allowed businesses of all sizes to advertise on Google's search results.

milestones.title: Eric Schmidt Joins as CEO
milestones.description: Eric Schmidt becomes CEO, bringing executive experience to the growing company.
milestones.longDescription: Eric Schmidt joined Google as CEO in August 2001, forming the famous triumvirate with Larry Page and Sergey Brin. He would lead the company through its explosive growth phase until 2011.

milestones.title: AdSense Program Launched
milestones.description: Google AdSense allows website publishers to display targeted ads and earn revenue.
milestones.longDescription: Google AdSense launched in March 2003, enabling website owners to monetize their content by displaying relevant ads. This created a new revenue stream for publishers and expanded Google's advertising reach.

milestones.title: Gmail Announced
milestones.description: Google announces Gmail with 1GB of storage, revolutionizing email services.
milestones.longDescription: Launched on April Fools' Day 2004, Gmail offered an unprecedented 1GB of free storage (500 times more than competitors) and introduced innovative features like conversation threading and powerful search.

milestones.title: Google Goes Public
milestones.description: Google's IPO raises $1.67 billion, valuing the company at $23 billion.
milestones.longDescription: Google's initial public offering on August 19, 2004, used a unique Dutch auction process. The IPO price was $85 per share, and the company raised $1.67 billion, making it one of the largest tech IPOs at the time.

milestones.title: Google Maps Launches
milestones.description: Google Maps introduces interactive, draggable maps with local search integration.
milestones.longDescription: Google Maps launched in February 2005, revolutionizing online mapping with AJAX-based draggable maps, satellite imagery, and tight integration with Google Search for local businesses.

milestones.title: Personalized Search Introduced
milestones.description: Google begins personalizing search results based on user search history.
milestones.longDescription: Google introduced personalized search in June 2005, using signed-in users' search history to tailor results. This marked the beginning of Google's move toward more customized search experiences.

milestones.title: YouTube Acquisition
milestones.description: Google acquires YouTube for $1.65 billion, expanding into video search and content.
milestones.longDescription: In October 2006, Google acquired YouTube for $1.65 billion in stock, gaining the leading video-sharing platform and significantly enhancing its video search capabilities and content ecosystem.

milestones.title: Universal Search Launched
milestones.description: Google integrates images, videos, news, and maps into main search results.
milestones.longDescription: Universal Search, launched in May 2007, blended results from Google's various search engines (Images, News, Videos, Maps) into a single results page, creating a more comprehensive search experience.

milestones.title: Google Suggest Becomes Default
milestones.description: Auto-complete suggestions become a standard feature for all users.
milestones.longDescription: Google Suggest (later called Autocomplete) became a default feature in August 2008, predicting search queries as users type and significantly improving search efficiency and discovery.

milestones.title: Caffeine Infrastructure Preview
milestones.description: Google announces Caffeine, a complete rewrite of the search indexing system.
milestones.longDescription: Caffeine was previewed in August 2009 as a complete overhaul of Google's indexing infrastructure, designed to provide 50% fresher results and index the web continuously rather than in batches.

milestones.title: Google Instant Launches
milestones.description: Search results appear dynamically as users type their queries.
milestones.longDescription: Google Instant, launched in September 2010, displayed search results in real-time as users typed. This feature aimed to save 2-5 seconds per search by predicting queries and showing results before users finished typing.

milestones.title: Panda Algorithm Update
milestones.description: Major algorithm update targets low-quality content and content farms.
milestones.longDescription: The Panda update, released in February 2011, was designed to lower the rank of low-quality sites and return higher-quality sites near the top of search results. It affected 12% of all search queries.

milestones.title: Google+ Integration
milestones.description: Google launches Google+ and begins integrating social signals into search.
milestones.longDescription: Google+ launched in June 2011 as Google's social network, with deep integration into search results through features like author profiles and social search results.

milestones.title: Knowledge Graph Introduced
milestones.description: Semantic search engine understands entities and relationships, not just keywords.
milestones.longDescription: The Knowledge Graph, launched in May 2012, represented a shift from strings to things. It provided instant answers and information about people, places, and things directly in search results.

milestones.title: Penguin Algorithm Update
milestones.description: Algorithm update targets webspam and manipulative link building practices.
milestones.longDescription: The Penguin update, released in April 2012, specifically targeted sites using black-hat SEO techniques like keyword stuffing and manipulative link schemes. It affected approximately 3.1% of search queries.

milestones.title: Hummingbird Algorithm
milestones.description: Complete algorithm rewrite focuses on semantic search and natural language.
milestones.longDescription: Hummingbird, announced in September 2013, was Google's largest algorithm change since 2001. It focused on understanding the meaning behind queries rather than just matching keywords, enabling better conversational search.

milestones.title: Mobile-Friendly Update
milestones.description: Major ranking boost for mobile-optimized pages, known as 'Mobilegeddon'.
milestones.longDescription: On April 21, 2015, Google rolled out a major update that made mobile-friendliness a ranking signal. This update, dubbed 'Mobilegeddon,' significantly boosted mobile-friendly pages in mobile search results.

milestones.title: RankBrain AI System
milestones.description: Machine learning AI system helps process search queries and understand intent.
milestones.longDescription: RankBrain, announced in October 2015, is a machine learning AI system that helps Google process search results and better understand queries. It became the third most important ranking factor.

milestones.title: Mobile-First Indexing Announced
milestones.description: Google announces plans to primarily use mobile version of content for indexing and ranking.
milestones.longDescription: In November 2016, Google announced mobile-first indexing, meaning Google would predominantly use the mobile version of content for indexing and ranking, reflecting the shift to mobile-majority internet usage.

milestones.title: Featured Snippets Expansion
milestones.description: Google expands featured snippets, providing instant answers at position zero.
milestones.longDescription: Featured snippets, which extract and display content directly in search results at 'position zero,' saw major expansion in 2017, fundamentally changing how information is presented in search.

milestones.title: BERT Algorithm Introduction
milestones.description: Neural network-based technique helps Google better understand natural language context.
milestones.longDescription: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers), introduced in November 2018, helps Google better understand the context of words in searches. It affected 10% of all queries and represented the biggest leap forward in search in five years.

milestones.title: Google Discover Enhanced
milestones.description: Personalized content feed shows relevant articles without requiring a search query.
milestones.longDescription: Google Discover, the personalized content feed, received major enhancements in 2019, using AI to surface relevant content based on user interests without requiring explicit searches.

milestones.title: Core Web Vitals Announced
milestones.description: Google introduces page experience signals measuring loading, interactivity, and visual stability.
milestones.longDescription: Core Web Vitals, announced in May 2020, are a set of metrics measuring real-world user experience for loading performance, interactivity, and visual stability. They became ranking factors in 2021.

milestones.title: MUM (Multitask Unified Model)
milestones.description: AI model 1000x more powerful than BERT, understanding and generating language across tasks.
milestones.longDescription: MUM (Multitask Unified Model), introduced in May 2021, is an AI model that's 1,000 times more powerful than BERT. It can understand information across text and images and works in 75 languages.

milestones.title: Page Experience Ranking Update
milestones.description: Core Web Vitals become official ranking factors alongside mobile-friendliness and HTTPS.
milestones.longDescription: In June 2021, page experience signals including Core Web Vitals, mobile-friendliness, safe-browsing, HTTPS, and intrusive interstitial guidelines became official ranking factors.

milestones.title: Helpful Content Update
milestones.description: Algorithm update rewards content created for people, not search engines.
milestones.longDescription: The Helpful Content Update, launched in August 2022, aims to reward content created primarily to help people rather than content designed to rank well in search engines.

milestones.title: Bard AI Announced
milestones.description: Google announces Bard, an experimental conversational AI service powered by LaMDA.
milestones.longDescription: In March 2023, Google launched Bard, an experimental conversational AI service powered by LaMDA, in response to ChatGPT. This marked Google's entry into the generative AI chatbot space.

milestones.title: Search Generative Experience (SGE)
milestones.description: AI-powered search experience generates comprehensive answers using generative AI.
milestones.longDescription: Search Generative Experience (SGE), announced at Google I/O 2023, uses generative AI to provide AI-powered snapshots of key information with links to dig deeper. It represents a fundamental reimagining of search.

milestones.title: Gemini Integration
milestones.description: Google's most capable AI model integrates into search for enhanced understanding.
milestones.longDescription: Google integrated its most capable AI model, Gemini, into search in February 2024, enabling more sophisticated understanding of complex queries and multimodal search capabilities.

milestones.title: AI Overviews Launch
milestones.description: AI-generated summaries appear at the top of search results for billions of queries.
milestones.longDescription: AI Overviews (formerly SGE) became widely available in May 2024, providing AI-generated summaries at the top of search results, fundamentally changing how users interact with search results.

milestones.title: Advanced Multimodal Search
milestones.description: Search seamlessly combines text, image, video, and voice inputs for unified understanding.
milestones.longDescription: By 2025, Google Search has evolved to seamlessly handle multimodal queries, allowing users to search using any combination of text, images, video, and voice, with AI understanding context across all modalities.
